ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "global_rank" INTEGER /* Global rank of the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region the country belongs to */,
  "subregion_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subregion the country belongs to */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Name of the capital city */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language of the country */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Official currency of the country */,
  "government_type" TEXT /* Type of government */,
  "area" INTEGER /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "population_estimate" INTEGER /* Estimated population (for recent data) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date of the last data update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was recorded */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "yearly_percent_change" REAL /* Yearly percentage change in population */,
  "yearly_change" INTEGER /* Yearly change in population */,
  "migrants_net" INTEGER /* Net number of migrants */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "fertility_rate" REAL /* Fertility rate of the population */,
  "density" INTEGER /* Population density per square kilometer */,
  "urban_pop_percent" REAL /* Percentage of urban population */,
  "urban_population" INTEGER /* Total urban population */,
  "birth_rate" REAL /* Birth rate per 1,000 population */,
  "death_rate" REAL /* Death rate per 1,000 population */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Average life expectancy at birth */,
  "gdp_per_capita" REAL /* GDP per capita */,
  "literacy_rate" REAL /* Literacy rate of the population */,
  "health_expenditure" REAL /* Health expenditure as a percentage of GDP */,
  "education_expenditure" REAL /* Education expenditure as a percentage of GDP */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "world_population" (
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was recorded */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total world population */,
  "countries_share" REAL /* Percentage share of the country's population in the world population */,
  "birth_rate" REAL /* Global birth rate per 1,000 population */,
  "death_rate" REAL /* Global death rate per 1,000 population */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Global average life expectancy at birth */,
  "gdp_total" REAL /* Total global GDP */,
  "gdp_per_capita" REAL /* Global GDP per capita */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_world_population_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the trend belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the trend was recorded */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Name of the population metric (e.g., population, fertility rate, urbanization) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the population metric */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_trends_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_trends_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date of the last update to the data source */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data source (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)
column_names: ID of the region the country belongs to
ID of the subregion the country belongs to
Total area of the country in square kilometers
